channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: One of Intel’s fastest desktop CPUs is $50 off
item.description: The Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus was billed by the company in late March as one of its “fastest gaming desktop processor ever.” That hype was evidently accurate, as benchmarks done by Gamers Nexus...

item.title: Cron Jobs in Node.js: The Practical Guide Nobody Gave Me
item.description: Cron Jobs in Node.js: The Practical Guide Nobody Gave Me I spent 3 days learning things about cron in Node.js that should have been documented somewhere. Here is everything I wish I had known. The...

item.title: I Reverse-Engineered NYC Local Law 97. Here's What 40% of Buildings Are Getting Wrong.
item.description: LL97 Penalties Aren’t What Most Calculators Show The $268/Tonne Problem NYC Local Law 97 is one of the most aggressive building performance standards in the United States. Buildings larger than...

item.title: U.S. DOJ demands Apple and Google unmask over 100k users of car-tinkering app
item.description: Article URL: https://macdailynews.com/2026/05/15/u-s-doj-demands-apple-and-google-unmask-over-100000-users-of-popular-car-tinkering-app-in-emissions-crackdown/ Comments URL:...

item.title: I Audited 70 Companies' llms.txt Files. Most Don't Have One.
item.description: I Audited 70 Companies' llms.txt Files. Most Don't Have One. The /llms.txt proposal has been around since late 2024. Two years in, every SEO blog has a tutorial on it. Every "GEO" thinkpiece...

item.title: 🧠 Beyond Chatbots: Understanding Hermes Agent and the Rise of Autonomous AI Systems
item.description: This is a submission for the Hermes Agent Challenge 🏆 Why I Started Exploring Hermes Agent Most AI applications today still depend on a simple interaction pattern: 👉 User asks 👉 Model responds 👉...

item.title: XGBoost: When Gradient Boosting Meets Regularization
item.description: 1. The Problem It Solves Imagine you’re a loan officer at a bank. You have thousands of past loan applications with features like income, credit score, employment length, and debt-to-income ratio....

item.title: I Turned a Free API Into a 00/Month Side Project
item.description: I Turned a Free API Into a $200/Month Side Project The API existed. People needed it. They just didn't want to figure it out themselves. The Origin Story Last year I was building a crypto tracking...

item.title: Why Web Agents Fail on Protected Sites — And How to Fix It at the Infrastructure Level
item.description: Web agents are increasingly central to how AI systems interact with the web — automating research, extracting structured data, completing multi-step workflows. But in production, many of them...

item.title: BGP Edge Hygiene at a PCI-Regulated Fintech: IRR + RPKI in Production
item.description: A single hijacked prefix can route a chunk of payment traffic into a stranger's network for half an hour before anyone notices. For a payment provider, that is not a routing incident. It is a...

item.title: Cron Jobs in Node.js: The Practical Guide Nobody Gave Me
item.description: Cron Jobs in Node.js: The Practical Guide Nobody Gave Me I spent 3 hours debugging why my cron job only ran once. Here's everything I wish I'd known before starting. Why This Matters Every serious...

item.title: Turla Turns Kazuar Backdoor Into Modular P2P Botnet for Persistent Access
item.description: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group known as Turla has transformed its custom backdoor Kazuar into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet that's engineered for stealth and persistent access to...

item.title: Greg Brockman Officially Takes Control of OpenAI’s Products in Latest Shakeup
item.description: OpenAI is once again reorganizing its executive ranks as part of its effort to unify ChatGPT and Codex into one core product experience.

item.title: AI radio hosts demonstrate why AI can’t be trusted alone
item.description: Andon Labs has been running a series of experiments in which AI agents run businesses without human intervention. Its latest is a quartet of radio stations run by some of the most popular AI...

item.title: Does Trump Mobile know how many stripes are on the American flag?
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This week, despite our best hopes, we still don't...

item.title: Google updates its spam rules to include attempts to ‘manipulate’ AI
item.description: Google updated its spam policy to mark attempts to "manipulate" its AI model in search results as spam, including results in AI Overview or AI Mode in Search, as Search Engine Land reports: "In...

item.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts
item.description: Once users connect their accounts, they will see a dashboard of their portfolio performance, spending, subscriptions, and upcoming payments.

item.title: OpenAI now wants ChatGPT to access your bank accounts
item.description: Your trust in AI is about to be put to the test: OpenAI will soon let you give the chatbot direct access to your bank accounts. The new feature announced in preview today will allow users to...

item.title: BioLite’s stakeable solar lights are down to a new low price for Memorial Day
item.description: The Memorial Day deals have arrived — err, at least some of them. BioLite, purveyors of all things solar, has kicked off a larger holiday promo ahead of May 26th, resulting in reduced pricing on...

item.title: The funniest thing about the Trump arcade game is how good it is
item.description: This week, a trio of video game cabinets emblazoned with pixel art of Donald Trump and a number of other government officials' faces suddenly appeared at the DC War Memorial. Looking at the...

item.title: Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI
item.description: AI video-generation startup Runway is betting that video generation is the path to world models. And that being an AI outsider is an advantage, not a liability.

item.title: The promises and pitfalls of personalized health
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your...

item.title: Four OpenClaw Flaws Enable Data Theft, Privilege Escalation, and Persistence
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a set of four security flaws in OpenClaw that could be chained to achieve data theft, privilege escalation, and persistence. The vulnerabilities,...

item.title: X is fighting Andrew Tate’s attempt to unmask his critics
item.description: X is fighting for its users' right to anonymity against the far-right influencers and accused human traffickers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are seeking to unmask their online critics. The Tate...

item.title: Bose’s new Lifestyle Ultra speakers are now available
item.description: Sonos has a new rival in town. Bose’s new Lifestyle Collection includes a $299 Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, a $1,099 Lifestyle Ultra Soundbar, and the $899 Ultra Subwoofer, all of which are now...

item.title: Osaurus brings both local and cloud AI models to your Mac
item.description: Osaurus￼ combines local and cloud AI models in a Mac app that keeps users’ memory, files, and tools on their own hardware.

item.title: The Download: China’s AI drama factory and the WHO’s missing health targets
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How Chinese short dramas became AI content machines...

item.title: Bypassing On-Camera Age-Verification Checks
item.description: Some AI-based video age-verification checks can be fooled with a fake mustache.

item.title: What 45 Days of Watching Your Own Tools Will Tell You About Your Real Attack Surface
item.description: In Your Biggest Security Risk Isn't Malware — It's What You Already Trust, we made a simple argument: the most dangerous activity inside most organizations no longer looks like an attack. It looks...

item.title: TanStack Supply Chain Attack Hits Two OpenAI Employee Devices, Forces macOS Updates
item.description: OpenAI has disclosed that two of its employee devices in its corporate environment were impacted via the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack on TanStack, but noted that no user data, production...
